title: antillanthus carinatus
intro: Antillanthus carinatus, also known as the Santa Cruz Island tarweed, is a plant species that belongs to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, Antillanthus carinatus has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating various health issues. The plant is rich in essential oils and has been used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as skin irritations and wounds, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Antillanthus carinatus can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
